content:
Projects got me started, but systems made my code scalable, reusable, and actually valuable.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I had a folder on my laptop called Python Projects.
It looked impressive.
There were web scrapers. Chatbots. APIs. Dashboards. Automation scripts. AI experiments. Even a couple of machine learning models I was ridiculously proud of.
Whenever someone asked what I had built, I’d proudly open that folder and start scrolling.
Hundreds of files.
Dozens of Git repositories.
Thousands of lines of code.
Then one afternoon, a client came back after six months and asked for a small feature.
“Can you update the automation so it works with our new workflow?”
I smiled.
“Of course.”
Five minutes later, I opened the project.
Ten minutes later, I had no idea how it worked.
An hour later, I realized something embarrassing.
I hadn’t built software.
